structured_payload_candidate.title: Override Capability for Points
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Values for all points used in control sequences shall be capable of being overridden by the user with appropriate access level for testing and commissioning. If hardware design prevents this, equate to a software point.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Allow override of all points; use software points if hardware cannot be overridden.
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: Verify that all points can be overridden by authorized users.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Values for all points, including real (hardware) points used in control sequences shall be capable of being overridden by the user with appropriate access level (e.g., for testing and commissioning). If hardware design prevents this for hardware points, they shall be equated to a software point, and the software point shall be used in all sequences.

evidence_quote: Values for all points, including real (hardware) points used in control sequences shall be capable of being overridden by the user with appropriate access level (e.g., for testing and commissioning). If hardware design prevents this for hardware points, they shall be equated to a software point, and the software point shall be used in all sequences.
evidence_text: Values for all points, including real (hardware) points used in control sequences shall be capable of being overridden by the user with appropriate access level (e.g., for testing and commissioning). If hardware design prevents this for hardware points, they shall be equated to a software point, and the software point shall be used in all sequences.
judge_reason: Override capability requirement for commissioning, supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Control Loop Enable/Disable to Prevent Windup
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Control loops shall be enabled and disabled based on the status of the system being controlled to prevent windup.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Enable or disable control loops based on system status to prevent windup.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Unless otherwise indicated, control loops shall be enabled and disabled based on the status of the system being controlled to prevent windup.

evidence_quote: Unless otherwise indicated, control loops shall be enabled and disabled based on the status of the system being controlled to prevent windup.
evidence_text: Unless otherwise indicated, control loops shall be enabled and disabled based on the status of the system being controlled to prevent windup.
judge_reason: Operational sequence rule for enabling/disabling control loops to prevent windup, grounded in evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Control Loop Initialization to Neutral
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When a control loop is enabled or reenabled, it and all its constituents shall be set initially to a neutral value.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set the loop and its constituents (proportional and integral terms) to a neutral value.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When a control loop is enabled or reenabled, it and all its constituents (such as the proportional and integral terms) shall be set initially to a neutral value.

evidence_quote: When a control loop is enabled or reenabled, it and all its constituents (such as the proportional and integral terms) shall be set initially to a neutral value.
evidence_text: When a control loop is enabled or reenabled, it and all its constituents (such as the proportional and integral terms) shall be set initially to a neutral value.
judge_reason: Operational sequence rule for initializing control loops to neutral, supported by evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Neutral Control Loop Condition
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A control loop in neutral shall correspond to a condition that applies the minimum control effect (valves/dampers closed, VFDs at minimum speed).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Apply minimum control effect: valves/dampers closed, VFDs at minimum speed.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: A control loop in neutral shall correspond to a condition that applies the minimum control effect, i.e., valves/dampers closed, VFDs at minimum speed, etc.

evidence_quote: A control loop in neutral shall correspond to a condition that applies the minimum control effect, i.e., valves/dampers closed, VFDs at minimum speed, etc.
evidence_text: A control loop in neutral shall correspond to a condition that applies the minimum control effect, i.e., valves/dampers closed, VFDs at minimum speed, etc.
judge_reason: Operational definition of neutral control loop condition, supported by evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Outdoor Air Temperature Sensor Selection
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Systems with multiple outdoor air temperature sensors
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When there are multiple outdoor air temperature sensors, the system shall use the valid sensor that most accurately represents the outdoor air conditions at the equipment being controlled.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Multiple outdoor air temperature sensors available
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Use the valid sensor that most accurately represents outdoor air conditions at the equipment.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When there are multiple outdoor air temperature sensors, the system shall use the valid sensor that most accurately represents the outdoor air conditions at the equipment being controlled.

evidence_quote: When there are multiple outdoor air temperature sensors, the system shall use the valid sensor that most accurately represents the outdoor air conditions at the equipment being controlled.
evidence_text: When there are multiple outdoor air temperature sensors, the system shall use the valid sensor that most accurately represents the outdoor air conditions at the equipment being controlled.
judge_reason: Operational rule for selecting outdoor air temperature sensor, supported by evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Outdoor Air Temperature Sensor Validity for Air-Handler Intakes
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Outdoor air temperature sensors at air-handler outdoor air intakes shall be considered valid only when the supply fan is proven ON and the unit is in Occupied Mode or any other mode with the economizer enabled.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Supply fan proven ON and unit in Occupied Mode or economizer enabled
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Consider sensor valid only under those conditions.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Outdoor air temperature sensors at air-handler outdoor air intakes shall be considered valid only when the supply fan is proven ON and the unit is in Occupied Mode or in any other mode with the economizer enabled.

evidence_quote: Outdoor air temperature sensors at air-handler outdoor air intakes shall be considered valid only when the supply fan is proven ON and the unit is in Occupied Mode or in any other mode with the economizer enabled.
evidence_text: Outdoor air temperature sensors at air-handler outdoor air intakes shall be considered valid only when the supply fan is proven ON and the unit is in Occupied Mode or in any other mode with the economizer enabled.
judge_reason: Specific validity condition for outdoor air temperature sensors at intakes, supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Outdoor Air Temperature Averaging for Global Sequences
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Global sequences (optimum start, plant lockout, etc.)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The outdoor air temperature used for optimum start, plant lockout, and other global sequences shall be the average of all valid sensor readings. If there are four or more valid sensors, discard the highest and lowest readings.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Global sequence requiring outdoor air temperature
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Average all valid sensor readings; if four or more, discard highest and lowest.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The outdoor air temperature used for optimum start, plant lockout, and other global sequences shall be the average of all valid sensor readings. If there are four or more valid outdoor air temperature sensors, discard the highest and lowest temperature readings.

evidence_quote: The outdoor air temperature used for optimum start, plant lockout, and other global sequences shall be the average of all valid sensor readings. If there are four or more valid outdoor air temperature sensors, discard the highest and lowest temperature readings.
evidence_text: The outdoor air temperature used for optimum start, plant lockout, and other global sequences shall be the average of all valid sensor readings. If there are four or more valid outdoor air temperature sensors, discard the highest and lowest temperature readings.
judge_reason: Method for averaging outdoor air temperature for global sequences, supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Proven Status Definition
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: All equipment with DI status points
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The term 'proven' (proven ON/proven OFF) means that the equipment's DI status point matches the state set by the equipment's DO command point.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Match DI status to DO command state.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The term “proven” (i.e., “proven ON”/“proven OFF”) shall mean that the equipment’s DI status point (where provided, e.g., current switch, DP switch, or VFD status) matches the state set by the equipment’s DO command point.

evidence_quote: The term “proven” (i.e., “proven ON”/“proven OFF”) shall mean that the equipment’s DI status point (where provided, e.g., current switch, DP switch, or VFD status) matches the state set by the equipment’s DO command point.
evidence_text: The term “proven” (i.e., “proven ON”/“proven OFF”) shall mean that the equipment’s DI status point (where provided, e.g., current switch, DP switch, or VFD status) matches the state set by the equipment’s DO command point.
judge_reason: Operational definition of 'proven' status, used in sequences, supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Proportional-Only Loops for Limiting Loops
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Use proportional-only (P-only) loops for limiting loops to prevent integral windup from causing overshoot.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Use proportional-only control; do not use integral action.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Use proportional only (P-only) loops for limiting loops (such as zone CO2 control loops, etc.).

evidence_quote: Use proportional only (P-only) loops for limiting loops (such as zone CO2 control loops, etc.).
evidence_text: Use proportional only (P-only) loops for limiting loops (such as zone CO2 control loops, etc.).
judge_reason: Rule to use P-only loops for limiting loops, supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Derivative Term Usage Restriction
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Do not use the derivative term on any loops unless field tuning is not possible without it.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Avoid derivative term unless absolutely necessary.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Do not use the derivative term on any loops unless field tuning is not possible without it.

evidence_quote: Do not use the derivative term on any loops unless field tuning is not possible without it.
evidence_text: Do not use the derivative term on any loops unless field tuning is not possible without it.
judge_reason: Restriction on derivative term usage, supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Adjustability of Setpoints, Timers, Deadbands, PID Gains
structured_payload_candidate.summary: All setpoints, timers, deadbands, PID gains, etc. listed in sequences shall be adjustable by the user with appropriate access level. Software points shall be used; fixed scalar numbers shall not be embedded except for physical constants and conversion factors.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Make all parameters adjustable via software points; avoid embedded fixed numbers.
configurable_values: All setpoints, timers, deadbands, PID gains
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: All setpoints, timers, deadbands, PID gains, etc. listed in sequences shall be adjustable by the user with appropriate access level whether indicated as adjustable in sequences or not. Software points shall be used for these variables. Fixed scalar numbers shall not be embedded in programs except for physical constants and conversion factors.

evidence_quote: All setpoints, timers, deadbands, PID gains, etc. listed in sequences shall be adjustable by the user with appropriate access level whether indicated as adjustable in sequences or not. Software points shall be used for these variables. Fixed scalar numbers shall not be embedded in programs except for physical constants and conversion factors.
evidence_text: All setpoints, timers, deadbands, PID gains, etc. listed in sequences shall be adjustable by the user with appropriate access level whether indicated as adjustable in sequences or not. Software points shall be used for these variables. Fixed scalar numbers shall not be embedded in programs except for physical constants and conversion factors.
judge_reason: Requirement for adjustability of setpoints and gains, supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Control Loop Output Rate of Change Limit
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The output of every control loop shall be capable of being limited by a user adjustable maximum rate of change, with a default of 25% per minute.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Limit output rate of change; default 25% per minute.
configurable_values: Maximum rate of change (default 25% per minute)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: To avoid abrupt changes in equipment operation, the output of every control loop shall be capable of being limited by a user adjustable maximum rate of change, with a default of 25% per minute.

evidence_quote: To avoid abrupt changes in equipment operation, the output of every control loop shall be capable of being limited by a user adjustable maximum rate of change, with a default of 25% per minute.
evidence_text: To avoid abrupt changes in equipment operation, the output of every control loop shall be capable of being limited by a user adjustable maximum rate of change, with a default of 25% per minute.
judge_reason: Parameter specification for control loop output rate of change, supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Maintenance Mode Alarm Suppression
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Operators shall have the ability to put any device in/out of maintenance mode. All alarms associated with a device in maintenance mode will be suppressed, except life safety alarms. Issue a daily Level 3 alarm if device remains in maintenance mode.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Suppress all alarms except life safety; issue daily Level 3 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Operators shall have the ability to put any device (e.g., AHU) in/out of maintenance mode. a. All alarms associated with a device in maintenance mode will be suppressed. Exception: Life safety alarms shall not be suppressed. b. If a device is in maintenance mode, issue a daily Level 3 alarm at a scheduled time indicating that the device is still in maintenance mode.

evidence_quote: Operators shall have the ability to put any device (e.g., AHU) in/out of maintenance mode. a. All alarms associated with a device in maintenance mode will be suppressed. Exception: Life safety alarms shall not be suppressed. b. If a device is in maintenance mode, issue a daily Level 3 alarm at a scheduled time indicating that the device is still in maintenance mode.
evidence_text: Operators shall have the ability to put any device (e.g., AHU) in/out of maintenance mode. a. All alarms associated with a device in maintenance mode will be suppressed. Exception: Life safety alarms shall not be suppressed. b. If a device is in maintenance mode, issue a daily Level 3 alarm at a scheduled time indicating that the device is still in maintenance mode.
judge_reason: Operational sequence for maintenance mode alarm suppression, supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Alarm Levels Definition
structured_payload_candidate.summary: There shall be 4 levels of alarm: Level 1 (life-safety), Level 2 (critical equipment), Level 3 (urgent), Level 4 (normal).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Define four alarm levels as specified.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: There shall be 4 levels of alarm a. Level 1: Life-safety message b. Level 2: Critical equipment message c. Level 3: Urgent message d. Level 4: Normal message

evidence_quote: There shall be 4 levels of alarm a. Level 1: Life-safety message b. Level 2: Critical equipment message c. Level 3: Urgent message d. Level 4: Normal message
evidence_text: There shall be 4 levels of alarm a. Level 1: Life-safety message b. Level 2: Critical equipment message c. Level 3: Urgent message d. Level 4: Normal message
judge_reason: Definition of four alarm levels, part of operational alarm framework, supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Post-Exit Suppression Period Defaults
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Default suppression periods: Level 1: 0 minutes, Level 2: 5 minutes, Level 3: 24 hours, Level 4: 7 days.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Apply default suppression periods after alarm exit.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Default suppression periods are as follows : a. Level 1 alarms: 0 minutes b. Level 2 alarms: 5 minutes c. Level 3 alarms: 24 hours d. Level 4 alarms: 7 days

evidence_quote: Default suppression periods are as follows : a. Level 1 alarms: 0 minutes b. Level 2 alarms: 5 minutes c. Level 3 alarms: 24 hours d. Level 4 alarms: 7 days
evidence_text: Default suppression periods are as follows : a. Level 1 alarms: 0 minutes b. Level 2 alarms: 5 minutes c. Level 3 alarms: 24 hours d. Level 4 alarms: 7 days
judge_reason: Default suppression periods directly quoted and supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: VFD Speed Point Configuration
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The speed AO sent to VFDs shall be configured such that 0% speed corresponds to 0 Hz, and 100% speed corresponds to maximum speed configured in the VFD.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Configure speed command so 0% = 0 Hz, 100% = max speed in VFD.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The speed AO sent to VFDs shall be configured such that 0% speed corresponds to 0 Hz, and 100% speed corresponds to maximum speed configured in the VFD.

evidence_quote: The speed AO sent to VFDs shall be configured such that 0% speed corresponds to 0 Hz, and 100% speed corresponds to maximum speed configured in the VFD.
evidence_text: The speed AO sent to VFDs shall be configured such that 0% speed corresponds to 0 Hz, and 100% speed corresponds to maximum speed configured in the VFD.
judge_reason: VFD speed point configuration rule, supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Equipment Staging and Rotation - Lead/Lag Alternation
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Identical parallel staged equipment (e.g., CHW pumps, cooling towers)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Lead/lag equipment shall be alternated when more than one is off or more than one is on so that equipment with most operating hours (Staging Runtime) is last stage and least hours is lead stage.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: More than one equipment off or more than one on
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Alternate lead/lag based on Staging Runtime: most hours last, least hours lead.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Unless otherwise noted, identical parallel staged equipment (such as CHW pumps and cooling towers) shall be lead/lag alternated when more than one is off or more than one is on so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the lead stage equipment.

evidence_quote: Unless otherwise noted, identical parallel staged equipment (such as CHW pumps and cooling towers) shall be lead/lag alternated when more than one is off or more than one is on so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the lead stage equipment.
evidence_text: Unless otherwise noted, identical parallel staged equipment (such as CHW pumps and cooling towers) shall be lead/lag alternated when more than one is off or more than one is on so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the lead stage equipment.
judge_reason: Staging and rotation rule for lead/lag alternation, supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan/Pump Fault Condition - Status Mismatch
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A fault condition exists if status point does not match its on/off point for 3 seconds after a time delay of 15 seconds while equipment is commanded on.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 2 alarm and trigger fault response.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Status point not matching on/off point for 3 seconds after 15-second delay while commanded on
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Status point not matching its on/off point for 3 seconds after a time delay of 15 seconds while the equipment is commanded on.

evidence_quote: Status point not matching its on/off point for 3 seconds after a time delay of 15 seconds while the equipment is commanded on.
evidence_text: Status point not matching its on/off point for 3 seconds after a time delay of 15 seconds while the equipment is commanded on.
judge_reason: Fan/pump status mismatch fault condition, supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Chiller Fault Condition - Safety Shutdown
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A fault condition exists if safety shutdown alarm condition is received either through network or hardwired alarm contact.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 2 alarm and trigger fault response.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Safety shutdown alarm condition either through network or hardwired alarm contact
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Safety shutdown alarm condition either through network or hardwired alarm contact

evidence_quote: Safety shutdown alarm condition either through network or hardwired alarm contact
evidence_text: Safety shutdown alarm condition either through network or hardwired alarm contact
judge_reason: Chiller safety shutdown fault condition, supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Boiler Fault Condition - Safety Shutdown
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A fault condition exists if safety shutdown alarm condition is received either through network or hardwired alarm contact.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 2 alarm and trigger fault response.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Safety shutdown alarm condition either through network or hardwired alarm contact
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Safety shutdown alarm condition either through network or hardwired alarm contact

evidence_quote: Safety shutdown alarm condition either through network or hardwired alarm contact
evidence_text: Safety shutdown alarm condition either through network or hardwired alarm contact
judge_reason: Boiler safety shutdown fault condition, supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Variable Speed Fan/Pump Deceleration Rate
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Variable speed fans and pumps shall have a deceleration rate of 1 Hz/second or slower set in BAS logic when disabled to prevent nuisance trips of operating equipment.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set deceleration rate to 1 Hz/second or slower.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Variable speed fans and pumps shall have a deceleration rate of 1 Hz/second or slower set in BAS logic when disabled to prevent nuisance trips of operating equipment (e.g., chillers).

evidence_quote: Variable speed fans and pumps shall have a deceleration rate of 1 Hz/second or slower set in BAS logic when disabled to prevent nuisance trips of operating equipment (e.g., chillers).
evidence_text: Variable speed fans and pumps shall have a deceleration rate of 1 Hz/second or slower set in BAS logic when disabled to prevent nuisance trips of operating equipment (e.g., chillers).
judge_reason: Deceleration rate requirement for variable speed fans/pumps, supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Lead/Standby Alternation for Non-Continuous Equipment
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel 100% redundant equipment not running continuously
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Lead/standby equipment shall be alternated when more than one is off so that equipment with most operating hours (Staging Runtime) is last stage and least hours is earlier stage.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Alternate lead/standby based on Staging Runtime.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Unless equipment runs continuously, parallel equipment that are 100% redundant shall be lead/standby alternated when more than one of the equipment is off so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the earlier stage equipment.

evidence_quote: Unless equipment runs continuously, parallel equipment that are 100% redundant shall be lead/standby alternated when more than one of the equipment is off so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the earlier stage equipment.
evidence_text: Unless equipment runs continuously, parallel equipment that are 100% redundant shall be lead/standby alternated when more than one of the equipment is off so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the earlier stage equipment.
judge_reason: Lead/standby alternation for non-continuous equipment, supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Lead/Standby Switch for Continuous Equipment
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If equipment runs continuously, lead/standby positions shall switch at an adjustable day of the week and time based on Staging Runtime; standby equipment shall first be started and proven on before former lead is shut off.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Start standby, prove on, then shut off former lead.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If equipment runs continuously, lead/standby positions shall switch at an adjustable day of the week and time (e.g., every Tuesday at 10:00 am) based on Staging Runtime; standby equipment shall first be started and proven on before former lead equipment is changed to standby and shut off.

evidence_quote: If equipment runs continuously, lead/standby positions shall switch at an adjustable day of the week and time (e.g., every Tuesday at 10:00 am) based on Staging Runtime; standby equipment shall first be started and proven on before former lead equipment is changed to standby and shut off.
evidence_text: If equipment runs continuously, lead/standby positions shall switch at an adjustable day of the week and time (e.g., every Tuesday at 10:00 am) based on Staging Runtime; standby equipment shall first be started and proven on before former lead equipment is changed to standby and shut off.
judge_reason: Lead/standby switch for continuous equipment, supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Chiller Fault Condition - Failure to Start
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A fault condition exists if chiller status remains off 5 minutes after command to start (only applies when chiller first starts).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 2 alarm and trigger fault response.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Chiller status remains off 5 minutes after command to start
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Chiller status remains off 5 minutes after command to start (note: this condition only applies when a chiller first starts, i.e., once status is proven, then status is no longer used as a fault condition because status will come and go if chiller cycles on low load)

evidence_quote: Chiller status remains off 5 minutes after command to start (note: this condition only applies when a chiller first starts, i.e., once status is proven, then status is no longer used as a fault condition because status will come and go if chiller cycles on low load)
evidence_text: Chiller status remains off 5 minutes after command to start (note: this condition only applies when a chiller first starts, i.e., once status is proven, then status is no longer used as a fault condition because status will come and go if chiller cycles on low load)
judge_reason: Chiller failure to start fault condition, supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Chiller Fault Condition - Low Delta-T
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A fault condition exists if for 10 minutes, chilled water return temperature is at least 3°C (5°F) above CHWST setpoint and delta-T across the chiller is less than 2°C (3°F).
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: CHWR temperature ≥ CHWST setpoint + 3°C and delta-T < 2°C for 10 minutes
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 2 alarm and trigger fault response.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: For 10 minutes, chilled water return temperature has been at least 3°C (5°F) above the CHWST setpoint, and delta-T across the chiller, as determined based on the difference between chilled water return temperature and chilled water supply temperature measured at the chiller (i.e., not common CHWST), has been less than 2°C (3°F).
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: For 10 minutes, chilled water return temperature has been at least 3°C (5°F) above the CHWST setpoint, and delta-T across the chiller, as determined based on the difference between chilled water return temperature and chilled water supply temperature measured at the chiller (i.e., not common CHWST), has been less than 2°C (3°F).

evidence_quote: For 10 minutes, chilled water return temperature has been at least 3°C (5°F) above the CHWST setpoint, and delta-T across the chiller, as determined based on the difference between chilled water return temperature and chilled water supply temperature measured at the chiller (i.e., not common CHWST), has been less than 2°C (3°F).
evidence_text: For 10 minutes, chilled water return temperature has been at least 3°C (5°F) above the CHWST setpoint, and delta-T across the chiller, as determined based on the difference between chilled water return temperature and chilled water supply temperature measured at the chiller (i.e., not common CHWST), has been less than 2°C (3°F).
judge_reason: Chiller low delta-T fault condition, supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Boiler Fault Condition - Low Delta-T
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A fault condition exists if boiler leaving water temperature remains 8.3°C (15°F) below setpoint for 15 minutes and delta-T across the boiler is less than 6°C (10°F).
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Leaving water temperature ≥ setpoint - 8.3°C and delta-T < 6°C for 15 minutes
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 2 alarm and trigger fault response.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: If boiler leaving water temperature remains 8.3°C (15°F) below setpoint for 15 minutes and delta-T across the boiler, as determined based on the difference between hot water supply temperature and hot water return temperature measured at the boiler (i.e., not common HWST), has been less than 6°C (10°F).
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If boiler leaving water temperature remains 8.3°C (15°F) below setpoint for 15 minutes and delta-T across the boiler, as determined based on the difference between hot water supply temperature and hot water return temperature measured at the boiler (i.e., not common HWST), has been less than 6°C (10°F).

evidence_quote: If boiler leaving water temperature remains 8.3°C (15°F) below setpoint for 15 minutes and delta-T across the boiler, as determined based on the difference between hot water supply temperature and hot water return temperature measured at the boiler (i.e., not common HWST), has been less than 6°C (10°F).
evidence_text: If boiler leaving water temperature remains 8.3°C (15°F) below setpoint for 15 minutes and delta-T across the boiler, as determined based on the difference between hot water supply temperature and hot water return temperature measured at the boiler (i.e., not common HWST), has been less than 6°C (10°F).
judge_reason: Boiler low delta-T fault condition, supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Hand Operation Detection for Fans and Pumps
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Hand operation is determined if status point does not match its ON/OFF point for 15 seconds after a time delay of 60 seconds when equipment is commanded OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set equipment to lead device and generate Level 4 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Status point not matching ON/OFF point for 15 seconds after 60-second delay when commanded OFF
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Status point not matching its ON/OFF point for 15 seconds after a time delay of 60 seconds when the equipment is commanded OFF.

evidence_quote: Status point not matching its ON/OFF point for 15 seconds after a time delay of 60 seconds when the equipment is commanded OFF.
evidence_text: Status point not matching its ON/OFF point for 15 seconds after a time delay of 60 seconds when the equipment is commanded OFF.
judge_reason: Hand operation detection rule, supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: ASHRAE 90.1-2019 Economizer High Limits
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Table of required high limits for different device types and climate zones per ASHRAE 90.1-2019.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Use specified high limit thresholds.
configurable_values: Fixed dry bulb: 24°C (75°F), 21°C (70°F), or 18°C (65°F) depending on climate zone
Fixed enthalpy + fixed dry bulb: hOA > 66 kJ/kg (28 Btu/lb) or TOA > 24°C (75°F)
Differential enthalpy + fixed dry bulb: hOA > hRA and TOA > 24°C (75°F)

structured_payload_candidate.title: Title 24-2019 Economizer High Limits
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Table of required high limits for different device types and California climate zones per Title 24-2019.

structured_payload_candidate.title: SystemOK Flag Definition
structured_payload_candidate.summary: SystemOK shall be TRUE when: system is proven ON, achieving setpoint for at least 5 minutes, and ready to serve load.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set SystemOK TRUE when all conditions met.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: SystemOK shall be TRUE when all of the following are true: a. The system is proven ON. b. The system is achieving its temperature and/or pressure setpoint(s) for at least 5 minutes c. The system is ready and able to serve its load

evidence_quote: SystemOK shall be TRUE when all of the following are true: a. The system is proven ON. b. The system is achieving its temperature and/or pressure setpoint(s) for at least 5 minutes c. The system is ready and able to serve its load
evidence_text: SystemOK shall be TRUE when all of the following are true: a. The system is proven ON. b. The system is achieving its temperature and/or pressure setpoint(s) for at least 5 minutes c. The system is ready and able to serve its load
judge_reason: SystemOK flag definition, supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Hierarchical Alarm Suppression Logic
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The BAS shall selectively suppress alarms for load components if SystemOK is FALSE for the source system. For cooling water systems, suppress only high-temperature alarms; for heating water systems, suppress only low-temperature alarms; for air-side systems, suppress all alarms.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Suppress specific alarm types for loads as defined.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The BAS shall selectively suppress (i.e., fail to announce; alarms may still be logged to a database) alarms for load components if SystemOK is FALSE for the source system that serves that load. a. If SystemOK is FALSE for a cooling water system (i.e., chiller, cooling tower, or associated pump), then only high-temperature alarms from the loads shall be suppressed. b. If SystemOK is FALSE for a heating water system (i.e., boiler or associated pump), then only low temperature alarms from the loads shall be suppressed. c. If SystemOK is FALSE for an air-side system (air handler, fan coil, VAV box, etc.), then all alarms from the loads shall be suppressed.

evidence_quote: The BAS shall selectively suppress (i.e., fail to announce; alarms may still be logged to a database) alarms for load components if SystemOK is FALSE for the source system that serves that load. a. If SystemOK is FALSE for a cooling water system (i.e., chiller, cooling tower, or associated pump), then only high-temperature alarms from the loads shall be suppressed. b. If SystemOK is FALSE for a heating water system (i.e., boiler or associated pump), then only low temperature alarms from the loads shall be suppressed. c. If SystemOK is FALSE for an air-side system (air handler, fan coil, VAV box, etc.), then all alarms from the loads shall be suppressed.
evidence_text: The BAS shall selectively suppress (i.e., fail to announce; alarms may still be logged to a database) alarms for load components if SystemOK is FALSE for the source system that serves that load. a. If SystemOK is FALSE for a cooling water system (i.e., chiller, cooling tower, or associated pump), then only high-temperature alarms from the loads shall be suppressed. b. If SystemOK is FALSE for a heating water system (i.e., boiler or associated pump), then only low temperature alarms from the loads shall be suppressed. c. If SystemOK is FALSE for an air-side system (air handler, fan coil, VAV box, etc.), then all alarms from the loads shall be suppressed.
judge_reason: Hierarchical alarm suppression logic, supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cascading Hierarchical Suppression
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Hierarchical suppression shall cascade through multiple levels of load-source relationship such that alarms at downstream loads shall also be suppressed.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Suppress alarms at all downstream loads.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: This hierarchical suppression shall cascade through multiple levels of load-source relationship such that alarms at downstream loads shall also be suppressed.

evidence_quote: This hierarchical suppression shall cascade through multiple levels of load-source relationship such that alarms at downstream loads shall also be suppressed.
evidence_text: This hierarchical suppression shall cascade through multiple levels of load-source relationship such that alarms at downstream loads shall also be suppressed.
judge_reason: Cascading hierarchical suppression, supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Alarms Never Suppressed
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Life/safety (Level 1) alarms, failure-to-start alarms, and failure-to-stop/hand alarms will never be suppressed.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Never suppress these alarm types.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The following types of alarms will never be suppressed by this logic: a. Life/safety and Level 1 alarms b. Failure-to-start alarms (i.e., equipment is commanded ON, but status point shows equipment to be OFF) c. Failure-to-stop/hand alarms (i.e., equipment is commanded OFF, but status point shows equipment to be ON)

evidence_quote: The following types of alarms will never be suppressed by this logic: a. Life/safety and Level 1 alarms b. Failure-to-start alarms (i.e., equipment is commanded ON, but status point shows equipment to be OFF) c. Failure-to-stop/hand alarms (i.e., equipment is commanded OFF, but status point shows equipment to be ON)
evidence_text: The following types of alarms will never be suppressed by this logic: a. Life/safety and Level 1 alarms b. structured_payload_candidate.title: Time-Based Suppression Delay for Thermal Zone Temperature Alarms
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Default time delay period for thermal zone temperature alarms: 18 minutes per °C (10 minutes per °F) of difference but no longer than 120 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Calculate delay based on temperature difference; cap at 120 minutes.
